title: Views table should open saved view rows
body:
The Views screen should default to a table of saved views instead of an empty explanatory panel.

Acceptance:
- Personal and shared sections render as table rows.
- Clicking a row opens the saved issue view.
- Owner and visibility metadata are visible without opening the view.

title: Create dialog should apply template defaults
body:
Selecting a template in the issue composer should apply the template defaults before the user submits the ticket.

Acceptance:
- Status priority labels and type are populated from the chosen template.
- User-entered title text is preserved.
- Clearing the template returns to manual defaults.

title: Persist actor user profile on first ticket write
body:
When a human actor creates their first ticket the repository should receive a matching shared user profile record in the users collection.

Acceptance:
- The ticket and profile are committed together.
- The profile uses the actor email as the stable identity.
- Repeated writes do not duplicate the profile.

title: Saved view filters should survive repository sync
body:
Saved views need to round trip through GitDB so another machine can pull the repository and use the same filtered view.

Acceptance:
- Query group and sort settings are restored after sync.
- Unknown optional display fields are ignored safely.
- Pinned shared views appear in navigation.

title: Issue detail metadata editors need optimistic disabled states
body:
The issue detail sidebar should prevent duplicate metadata writes while a status priority assignee label or due date update is in flight.

Acceptance:
- Controls remain readable while disabled.
- Failed updates restore the previous visible value.
- The user receives a concise error message.

title: Projection should list labels by archived state
body:
The local projection should support listing active labels separately from archived labels so creation pickers stay clean.

Acceptance:
- Active queries exclude archived labels by default.
- Archived queries return only archived labels.
- Text filtering still works in both modes.

title: API schemas should accept partial template defaults
body:
Template create and update API inputs should accept partial issue defaults without requiring a title or body.

Acceptance:
- Defaults can include status priority labels assignee and type.
- Invalid relation objects are rejected.
- Existing clients that omit defaults still pass.

title: Triage view should surface unassigned backlog
body:
Create a saved view for intake work so unsorted issues are visible before they are assigned or moved into active statuses.

Acceptance:
- The view filters backlog items with no assignee.
- Sorting puts oldest created issues first.
- The view is available from the default navigation set.

title: Repository history should include view and template changes
body:
Repository history should show metadata changes alongside issue changes so users can understand why navigation or defaults changed.

Acceptance:
- Saved view create update and archive events are visible.
- Template changes include the template name.
- Entries include actor time and snapshot id.

title: Default templates should seed into new repositories
body:
New repositories should start with practical issue templates for bugs features implementation tasks initiatives and QA follow-up.

Acceptance:
- Templates are committed as repository metadata.
- Defaults align with existing statuses and priorities.
- Users can edit or disable seeded templates.

title: Blocked work filter should include inverse relations
body:
A view filter for blocked work should match issues that are either blocked by another issue or have an inverse blocking relation.

Acceptance:
- Relation queries can find both sides of a blocking relationship.
- Duplicate and related relations are not included.
- Results remain stable when sorted by priority.

title: Views page should support keyboard row activation
body:
Saved view rows should behave like selectable table rows for keyboard users.

Acceptance:
- Tab focuses the row action target.
- Enter opens the selected view.
- Focus styling is visible in dark mode.

title: CSV importer should reject missing required titles
body:
The sample ticket importer should fail early when a CSV row omits a title or body.

Acceptance:
- The error includes the CSV row number.
- Dry run performs the same validation.
- No tickets are written after validation fails.

title: Sample data should create label definitions before tickets
body:
The sample importer should create label metadata before writing tickets that reference those labels.

Acceptance:
- Labels have stable normalized ids.
- Re-running the importer updates label definitions safely.
- Ticket rows can use semicolon or pipe separated labels.

title: Issue list should show label colors from projection
body:
Issue rows should use projected label definitions for display names and colors instead of only showing raw label ids.

Acceptance:
- Known labels render with their configured color.
- Unknown labels still render as readable fallback pills.
- Rows do not shift height when labels load.

title: Initiative progress should count completed estimates
body:
Initiative progress summaries should include completed issue count and completed estimate totals.

Acceptance:
- Done child issues contribute to completed totals.
- Archived children are excluded from active progress.
- Missing estimates count as zero.

title: View detail should hide nested saved-view selector
body:
When the user is already inside a saved view the page should not show another saved-view selector that changes the same context.

Acceptance:
- The top-level title reflects the active view.
- Filtering controls stay available.
- Navigating back returns to the saved views table.

title: User picker should use shared profile display names
body:
Assignee pickers should show user profile display names from repository metadata while storing the stable user id.

Acceptance:
- Known users show display name and email.
- Unknown assignee ids are still selectable as plain text.
- Disabled users are hidden from default assignment suggestions.

title: Projection migration should add metadata tables safely
body:
Projection setup should include metadata tables for users labels saved views templates initiatives and records without breaking existing repositories.

Acceptance:
- Existing issue projection tests still pass.
- Empty metadata collections produce empty pages.
- Rebuilding projection from GitDB is deterministic.

title: Archive label should hide it from creation pickers
body:
Archiving a label should remove it from issue creation and update pickers without removing it from existing issues.

Acceptance:
- Existing issues continue to display the archived label.
- New issue pickers exclude archived labels.
- Archived labels can be restored later.

title: Template picker should preserve custom title text
body:
Opening the template picker after typing a custom title should not overwrite the title unless the user explicitly applies a title template.

Acceptance:
- Body template content can be inserted independently.
- Existing typed title remains visible.
- Applying a template is reversible before submit.

title: Search box should combine with active saved view query
body:
Search entered inside a saved view should narrow that view rather than replacing the saved query.

Acceptance:
- View filters remain active while text search is applied.
- Clearing search returns to the saved view result set.
- The current search text is not persisted unless the user saves a new view.

title: GitDB document ids should accept email filenames
body:
The GitDB identifier schema should allow email-shaped filenames so user records can use email addresses directly as document ids.

Acceptance:
- User profile ids can include at signs and dots.
- Unsafe path characters remain rejected.
- Existing issue ids continue to validate.

title: Issue record activity should separate comments and updates
body:
The issue activity feed should distinguish user comments from metadata updates and system provenance records.

Acceptance:
- Comments render as primary conversation entries.
- Status and priority changes render as compact activity rows.
- Hidden records stay out of the default feed.

title: Desktop build should verify renderer metadata routes
body:
The desktop package should have coverage for renderer queries and mutations that call metadata API routes.

Acceptance:
- Saved view query hooks call the correct API routes.
- Template and label mutations invalidate relevant cached queries.
- Tests can run without a live desktop window.
